title: T-009 — PM schedule due engine worker (pm_schedule_due_engine_v1, calibration_expiry_alert_v1, spare_parts_reorder_alert_v1) (MNT §8.1)
prompt:
# T-009 — PM/calibration/reorder cron worker

PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md §8.1 (3 cron rules), §9.4, §9.13, §9.9, §11.6 (V-MNT-22)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement the three daily cron jobs in `apps/worker` (Wave1 T-111 outbox+worker primitive) that drive PM due, calibration expiry, and spare reorder alerts.

## Pre-flight
Verify `apps/worker` scaffold from Wave1 T-111/T-112; verify it uses `app.set_org_context` per-tenant loop pattern. Verify `maintenance_outbox_events` table or unified `outbox_events` table location.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/worker/src/maintenance/pm-schedule-due-engine.ts`:
   - Daily run (cron registered in apps/worker/cron.ts). For each tenant, set org context, SELECT FROM maintenance_schedules WHERE active AND next_due_date <= today + warning_days; INSERT INTO maintenance_work_orders (state='open', source='pm_schedule', type from schedule_type, equipment_id, schedule_id, assigned_to_user_id from schedule.assigned_technician_id, priority='medium'); UPDATE maintenance_schedules SET last_completed_at=now() is NOT done here — that is on MWO completion (T-010).
2. `apps/worker/src/maintenance/calibration-expiry-alert.ts`:
   - Daily run. Scan latest calibration_records.next_due_date per instrument; emit notifications (insert into `notification_events` per Foundation pattern) staged 30d/7d/overdue per V-MNT-13.
3. `apps/worker/src/maintenance/spare-reorder-alert.ts`:
   - Daily run. SELECT FROM spare_parts_stock WHERE qty_on_hand<=reorder_point AND active; emit `notification_events` (warn severity) + write outbox event `spare_parts.reorder_alert` for downstream consumers; P2 D365 PO push deferred (BL-MAINT-07).
4. Each worker job is idempotent: per-day per-(equipment|instrument|part) dedup via INSERT with `ON CONFLICT (idempotency_key) DO NOTHING` where `idempotency_key = format('mwo:pm:%s:%s', schedule_id, today)`.
5. Vitest using a fake `now()` + Testcontainers Postgres asserting: PM schedule due in 5 days fires MWO when warning_days=7; calibration due in 7 days emits one notification with severity=warn; spare qty 10 with reorder_point=20 emits exactly one reorder alert per day.

## Files
- apps/worker/src/maintenance/pm-schedule-due-engine.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/maintenance/calibration-expiry-alert.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/maintenance/spare-reorder-alert.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/maintenance/__tests__/pm-engine.test.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/maintenance/__tests__/calibration-alert.test.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/maintenance/__tests__/spare-reorder.test.ts [create]
- apps/worker/cron.ts [modify — register the 3 schedules]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a maintenance_schedules row with next_due_date=today+5, warning_days=7, when PM engine runs, then exactly one maintenance_work_orders row (state='open', source='pm_schedule') is created with the equipment_id and schedule_id.
2. Given the engine runs twice on the same day, when checked, then only one MWO exists for that schedule per day (idempotency_key dedup).
3. Given a calibration_records row with next_due_date=today+7, when calibration cron runs, then one notification_events row with severity=warn + maintenance.* template is created.
4. Given a spare_parts_stock row qty_on_hand=10 reorder_point=20, when reorder cron runs, then one notification_events row (warn) + one outbox event `spare_parts.reorder_alert` are emitted.

## Test strategy
- RED: three vitest files asserting each engine's contract before code exists.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test maintenance/`.
- Verify idempotency by running engine twice in test.

## Risk red lines
- Do not bypass `app.set_org_context` — per-tenant loop is mandatory (RLS would leak otherwise).
- Do not duplicate PM MWOs per day — use idempotency_key dedup.
- Do not push to D365 here — BL-MAINT-07 P2.
- Do not couple to oee_maintenance_trigger_v1 — that is P2 stub registered in T-007 only.
- Do not skip the `apps/worker` scaffold dependency — if Wave1 T-111 has not landed, mark this task BLOCKED and escalate.

pipeline_inputs.description: Implement 3 daily cron engines (PM due, calibration expiry, spare reorder) in apps/worker using foundation per-tenant loop + idempotency_key dedup.
pipeline_inputs.details: Each engine sets org context per tenant, scans relevant table, emits notification_events or MWO rows. Idempotency_key keyed on (rule, entity_id, day). Uses Wave1 T-111/T-112 apps/worker primitive. Calibration expiry alert engine calls @monopilot/e-sign foundation primitive (T-124) — signEvent with replay nonce + paired audit (retention='security') for sign-off attestation.
out_of_scope: Outbox publisher for MWO state changes (T-012)
acceptance_criteria: Given maintenance_schedules row due in 5 days with warning_days=7, when PM engine runs, then exactly one MWO row (state='open', source='pm_schedule') is created
Given the engine runs twice in same day, when checked, then only one MWO exists per schedule per day
Given calibration_records.next_due_date=today+7, when calibration cron runs, then one notification_events row with severity=warn is created
Given spare_parts_stock qty_on_hand=10 reorder_point=20, when reorder cron runs, then one notification + one outbox event `spare_parts.reorder_alert` are emitted
test_strategy: RED: three vitest files (pm-engine, calibration-alert, spare-reorder) asserting each contract
Verify idempotency by running engine twice in test and asserting single row
risk_red_lines: Do not bypass app.set_org_context per-tenant loop
Do not duplicate PM MWOs per day — idempotency_key dedup mandatory
Do not couple to oee_maintenance_trigger_v1 (P2 stub)
Block on apps/worker scaffold (Wave1 T-111) — escalate if absent
